question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer does not configure any gRPC server-side circuit breaker, allowing a sustained flood of fee estimation requests to exhaust the applicationTaskExecutor and cause the fee manager to return null or zero fee values to all clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a missing @Transactional(readOnly=true) annotation on a gRPC service method that injects the 'readOnly' TransactionOperations bean but also calls a non-annotated helper method that opens a new read-write transaction, allowing fee schedule data to be modified outside the design constraint?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a gRPC server-side request logging interceptor in grpcServerConfigurer to send fee estimation requests with crafted transaction bodies that are not audited, allowing undetected manipulation of fee calculation inputs?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where the PlatformTransactionManager injected into transactionOperationsReadOnly() is a ChainedTransactionManager that delegates to a non-read-only transaction manager for fee-related operations, causing the read-only flag to be silently ignored and fee data to be written?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer does not configure any gRPC server-side health check interceptor, allowing the attacker to send fee estimation requests to a degraded server instance that is serving stale fee data without the client being aware?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a Spring transaction isolation level mismatch where the 'readOnly' TransactionTemplate uses READ_COMMITTED isolation (default) and a concurrent fee schedule update is committed between two reads within the same transaction, causing the gRPC service to return inconsistent fee values that differ from the actual on-chain fee schedule?
